title: The Community Garden
summary: Ben, a new resident, meets his neighbour Rosa at a building garden meeting and learns the rules and routines of their shared vegetable plot.

body.speaker: Rosa
body.text: Hi Ben. Are you here for the garden meeting?

body.speaker: Ben
body.text: Yes! I moved in last month, so this is my first time here. What is this garden for?

body.speaker: Rosa
body.text: Anyone in the building can use it. We plant vegetables and flowers together. We share the work and the food.

body.speaker: Ben
body.text: That sounds great. What do you usually grow here?

body.speaker: Rosa
body.text: Last year we had tomatoes, beans, and some herbs. This year I want to try peppers. They grow well in summer.

body.speaker: Ben
body.text: I can bring some seeds from home. I have basil and mint. Do you think we need more herbs?

body.speaker: Rosa
body.text: Yes, definitely! We also need someone to water the garden on weekends. Can you do Sundays?

body.speaker: Ben
body.text: Sure. I am usually home on Sundays. Is there anything else I should know?

body.speaker: Rosa
body.text: Just one important rule — take only what you grow, and always leave the rest for others.

body.speaker: Ben
body.text: That is a good rule. I think I am going to love this garden.

body.speaker: Rosa
body.text: Welcome to the community, Ben! We are very happy to have you here.

annotations.meaningVi: các loại rau thơm / thảo mộc
chỉ lấy những gì bạn trồng

grammarNotes.title: Modal 'can' for ability and permission
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'can' cho cả khả năng lẫn sự cho phép: 'Anyone can use it' (cho phép) / 'I can bring some seeds' (khả năng).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'can' for both ability and permission: 'Anyone can use it' (permission) / 'I can bring some seeds' (ability).

translationVi:
Rosa: Chào Ben. Bạn đến đây để dự cuộc họp về khu vườn phải không?
Ben: Vâng! Tôi mới chuyển đến tháng trước, vì vậy đây là lần đầu tiên tôi đến đây. Khu vườn này dùng để làm gì?
Rosa: Bất kỳ ai trong tòa nhà đều có thể sử dụng. Chúng tôi cùng nhau trồng rau và hoa. Chúng tôi chia sẻ công việc và thức ăn.
Ben: Nghe hay đó. Thường thì bạn trồng gì ở đây?
Rosa: Năm ngoái chúng tôi có cà chua, đậu và một số rau thơm. Năm nay tôi muốn thử trồng ớt. Chúng phát triển tốt vào mùa hè.
Ben: Tôi có thể mang hạt giống từ nhà đến. Tôi có húng quế và bạc hà. Bạn có nghĩ chúng ta cần thêm rau thơm không?
Rosa: Có chứ, chắc chắn rồi! Chúng ta cũng cần ai đó tưới vườn vào cuối tuần. Bạn có thể làm vào Chủ nhật không?
Ben: Được chứ. Tôi thường ở nhà vào Chủ nhật. Còn điều gì tôi nên biết không?
Rosa: Chỉ một quy tắc quan trọng — chỉ lấy những gì bạn trồng, và luôn để phần còn lại cho người khác.
Ben: Đó là một quy tắc hay. Tôi nghĩ tôi sẽ rất thích khu vườn này.
Rosa: Chào mừng bạn đến với cộng đồng, Ben! Chúng tôi rất vui khi có bạn ở đây.

questions.prompt: Why is this Ben's first time at the meeting?
options: He just moved in last month
questions.explanation: 'I moved in last month, so this is my first time here.'
questions.hint: Find Ben's reason for being new.

questions.prompt: Who can use the community garden?
options: Only people on the ground floor
questions.explanation: 'Anyone in the building can use it.'

questions.prompt: What did they grow in the garden last year?
questions.explanation: 'Last year we had tomatoes, beans, and some herbs.'
questions.hint: Find Rosa's answer about last year.

questions.prompt: What does Rosa want to grow this year?
questions.explanation: 'This year I want to try peppers.'
questions.hint: What new vegetable does Rosa mention?

questions.prompt: What seeds does Ben offer to bring?
questions.hint: Find what Ben says he has at home.

questions.prompt: What does the garden need on weekends?
questions.explanation: 'We also need someone to water the garden on weekends.'
questions.hint: What does Rosa ask Ben to help with?
When does Ben say he is free?

questions.prompt: What is the one important rule in the garden?
questions.explanation: 'Take only what you grow, and always leave the rest for others.'

questions.prompt: How does Ben feel about the garden rule?
options: He thinks it is a good rule
questions.hint: Find Ben's reaction to the rule.

questions.prompt: What does Rosa say at the very end?
options: She gives Ben a list of rules
She welcomes Ben to the community
She asks about Ben's old home

cloze.template:
Rosa: Hi Ben. Are you here for the garden {{b1}}?
Ben: Yes! I moved in last {{b2}}, so this is my first time here. What is this garden for?
Rosa: Anyone in the building can use it. We plant {{b3}} and flowers together. We share the work and the food.
Ben: That sounds great. What do you usually {{b4}} here?
Rosa: Last year we had tomatoes, {{b5}}, and some herbs. This year I want to try peppers. They grow well in summer.
Ben: I can bring some {{b6}} from home. I have basil and {{b7}}. Do you think we need more herbs?
Rosa: Yes, definitely! We also need someone to {{b8}} the garden on weekends. Can you do Sundays?
Ben: Sure. I am usually home on Sundays. Is there anything else I should know?
Rosa: Just one important rule — take only what you grow, and always leave the {{b9}} for others.
Ben: That is a good rule. I think I am going to love this garden.
Rosa: {{b10}} to the community, Ben! We are very happy to have you here.

blanks.explanation: 'Are you here for the garden meeting?'
'Last year we had tomatoes, beans, and some herbs.'
'I can bring some seeds from home.'
'…someone to water the garden on weekends.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The garden has one rule: take only what you grow. How might this simple rule build trust between neighbours? Can a small shared space change how people in a building relate to each other?
